title: ononis natrix subsp. picta
intro: Ononis natrix subsp. picta, also known as the Painted Burnet, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the legume family (Fabaceae). It is a perennial herb with medicinal properties, primarily due to its alkaloid content, including ononin and ononin glycosides. The Painted Burnet has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to soothe and calm the body. In herbal preparations, the plant can be made into infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
